reasoning: The 4-week moving average of initial unemployment claims (seasonally adjusted) was 227,000 on 2025-11-08. Recent weeks have seen the average slowly drift downwards from a peak of nearly 240,000 in September (inflated by strikes and hurricanes) towards the 225,000-230,000 range. A statistical model analyzing the momentum and seasonal adjustments generated an estimated probability of 45% for an increase by November 16. As the forecast horizon lengthens over the next several months to early 2026, the absence of predictable short-term momentum and the fact that we are working with seasonally adjusted initial claims suggests that the series should approximately follow a random walk with only a slight long-term drift. Thus, uncertainty increases and the probability smoothly converges toward 50% for the subsequent resolution dates (December 2025 and February 2026).
